questions.question: Why is combining 5-HTP with an SSRI considered a medical emergency risk?

options.text: 5-HTP cancels out the SSRI's effects completely
5-HTP is a direct serotonin precursor, and combining it with an SSRI can cause serotonin syndrome — a potentially fatal condition
5-HTP causes liver damage when taken with any other supplement
The combination is only dangerous at extremely high doses of 5-HTP

questions.explanation: 5-HTP is a direct serotonin precursor that bypasses the rate-limiting enzyme. Combining it with SSRIs (which block serotonin reuptake) can produce excessive serotonergic activity, causing serotonin syndrome — a spectrum ranging from tremor and agitation to hyperthermia, seizures, and death. There is no safe dose of 5-HTP alongside serotonergic medications.

questions.question: How do SSRIs paradoxically contribute to treatment resistance through nutrient depletion?

options.text: SSRIs deplete B6, folate, and B12 — the very cofactors needed for serotonin synthesis — in over 30% of long-term users
SSRIs have no effect on any nutrient levels
SSRIs only deplete vitamin C, which is irrelevant to mood
SSRIs increase all B vitamin levels, causing toxicity

questions.explanation: Over 30% of long-term SSRI users show deficient or suboptimal levels of B6, folate, and/or B12. These vitamins are required cofactors for serotonin synthesis. The SSRI increases serotonin availability in the synapse, but the brain cannot synthesize enough new serotonin because the cofactors are depleted — potentially undermining the medication's own therapeutic effect.

questions.question: Why must lithium users maintain consistent sodium intake?

options.text: Sodium makes lithium work faster
Lithium is reabsorbed through sodium transporters in the kidney, so low-sodium conditions increase lithium retention and toxicity risk
Sodium prevents lithium from being absorbed in the gut
There is no relationship between sodium and lithium

questions.explanation: Lithium is reabsorbed in the kidney's proximal tubule through the same sodium transporters. When sodium intake decreases (low-salt diet, dehydration, excessive sweating), the kidney compensates by retaining more sodium — and more lithium along with it — raising lithium blood levels and increasing toxicity risk.

questions.question: Why must MAOI users avoid aged cheeses, cured meats, and fermented foods?

options.text: These foods reduce the effectiveness of MAOIs
These foods contain tyramine, which can accumulate when MAO is inhibited and trigger a dangerous hypertensive crisis
These foods cause stomach upset only when combined with MAOIs
MAOIs make these foods taste unpleasant

questions.explanation: MAOIs inhibit the enzyme that breaks down tyramine (an amino acid found in aged, fermented, and cured foods). When MAO is inhibited, tyramine accumulates and can trigger a hypertensive crisis — dangerously elevated blood pressure. This creates a paradox for nutritional psychiatry, as many gut-supportive fermented foods are exactly what MAOI users must avoid.

questions.question: What is the recommended inositol dose that the 2022 pilot study found safe alongside lithium?

options.text: 18 g/day — the same dose used for panic disorder
12 g/day
4 g/day
No dose of inositol is safe with lithium

questions.explanation: The 2022 pilot study found that inositol at 4 g/day was safe in combination with lithium, with no evidence of mood destabilization. This low dose may partially restore peripheral inositol function without undermining lithium's central mood-stabilizing mechanism. High-dose inositol (12-18 g/day) should NOT be assumed safe with lithium, as it may counteract lithium's therapeutic effect.

title: Medication-Nutrient Interactions: What You Must Know - Quiz
